entry.title: Social Interactions needs
entry.summary: I believe that people’s needs for social interactions have different levels. I know that I have way less need to be social or to be with others than most people around me. People usually want to be around others during mealtime or just socialise and talk anything, whereas I …
entry.content:
I believe that people’s needs for social interactions have different levels. I know that I have way less need to be social or to be with others than most people around me. People usually want to be around others during mealtime or just socialise and talk anything, whereas I prefer to spend most of my time alone. I enjoy having meals alone or with the company of a book, a piece of music or just my own thoughts. I also like the quiet company of someone I love, for example, my husband. I love to sit beside him during meals and not necessary talking, but just enjoying the silence. I love silence. I appreciate just to be with someone, without being involved in an interaction, that’s not a problem for me.

It is not that I never enjoy socialising with people, I do, but only within a small part of my time. When I was in college, I used to joke that I had a daily quota of social interactions, and when I reach this quota, I cannot interact anymore. I used to say that without realising how much truth was that. Today I understand that it is related to energy. I don’t have enough energy to be with people all the time. Interactions in ANY way demands a lot of energy from myself, and if I require too much from my energy resource, I will be drained and non-functional. I am learning how to manage my energy better now (maybe a subject to another post).

When I first came to Ireland to my internship, I came alone. I remember that I would go to work and initially all the time off work I had I would spend alone. It was like heaven! The funny part is that a lot of people back in Brazil (and even people from work) were worried that I would be depressed or too alone if I didn’t go out with other people. But it was absolutely fine! I was happy, good, in peace. It was perfect for me. During the week I would use my social energy to work with people, so during the weekend, I found pleasure and peace by being alone. I remember spending my weekend days on long walks, on Cafes reading or writing, I wrote a lot of letters for people I love. After some time I came to know people better and started to accept invitations to do something together. It was fun too, I had an amazing time with people. But, as I said, for me time with people must be in small and sparse doses.

That’s why I feel that having lunch with people all weekdays at work is too much for me. I feel so tired after interacting with people during lunch, I much more prefer to have my lunches alone or just with my husband. I am thinking about how to deal with this. Yet I haven’t found a solution. Don’t get me wrong, people from work are really nice and friendly and I like them. It is just too much for me, just that. Nothing personal. Especially when, how it is these days, there are several people together during lunch, all talking and making sounds… it is so demanding to deal with. One solution is to change my mealtime, but like I said before I am a creature of routine, I don’t like the idea of changing my mealtime to later or earlier. But anyway, I will next week try some strategies on how to hide from people during my lunchtime.

I am happy to talk about this, for me, it is a step more to self-acceptance. For too long I have dealt with myself thinking that I was wrong for being different. I am not, I am just the way I am, not better, not worse, just myself. It is good to embrace who we are. : )

entry.title: Sunday anxiety & coping strategies
entry.summary: As today is Sunday, I will talk about anxiety because since when I remember this is my “official day” for anxiety to grow. I always felt bad on Sundays because the next day would be Monday. My problem with Mondays is the unexpected environment waiting for me in which I …
entry.content:
As today is Sunday, I will talk about anxiety because since when I remember this is my “official day” for anxiety to grow. I always felt bad on Sundays because the next day would be Monday. My problem with Mondays is the unexpected environment waiting for me in which I will need to deal with people, using a lot of energy to that end.

Because I was always like that, I have developed some strategies to cope with this anxiety, otherwise, I would not leave home and function in my own life. I listed them below.

  1. Planning the week ahead. A huge part of my anxiety comes from the unexpected day/week ahead, so, for me, one of the most effective ways to cope with my anxiety is planning my week ahead. I reached a point in my life that I realised I NEED to know beforehand what I am going to do in the week to be functional. If I don’t plan my week before it starts, my level of anxiety is enormous and I will probably find an excuse to stay at home or even physically manifest some kind of bad thing to actually need to stay at home, not being able to even leave my bed. I tried a lot to resist my need to know things earlier, it didn’t take me anywhere. So I just decided to accept it: I am like this, I need planning in my life and that’s okay. In Brazil we have this saying “just accept it because it hurts less”. That’s exactly what I did, I accepted and started to consistently plan my week ahead. I am happier now.
  2. Following my weekdays’ routine during the weekend. I am a person who loves routine. I absolutely love do the same thing day after day after day… I just love that my days have the same structure, I need structure in my life. So another thing that troubled me during the weekend was the difference in the structure of the days. I usually struggle a lot with changes, so when it was Sunday, I started to suffer in anticipation because the next day would be different from the last one. I know it sounds weird, but that’s how my brain works, it doesn’t like things differently. So, what I started to do? To keep on my weekends the exactly the same structure from my weekdays and it does wonders do help me with my anxiety: I don’t need to suffer over my days being different anymore. I keep the same sleeping and waking time, my exercises hours, my blocks of deep work, and several other things (I will soon write a post about my current routine). I am aware that most of the people would find this tough to follow, but for me, it is necessary and actually makes me happier and calmer.
  3. Enjoying the day. This one seems obvious but if you are anxious, you will find hard to enjoy the present moment. I used to be like this, I would spend my whole Sunday suffering over the next day and I would just miss my day. So nowadays I consciously set an intention to enjoy my day. I enjoy that usually I don’t need to be around people on Sundays, apart from my husband but I love being in his presence, I enjoy having more time to read, to walk outside, to cook, to clean my house, to study things I love… I just enjoy the whole day, without thinking about the next day, it is already planned on my calendar and I know exactly how it will look, so I can take it off my mind.

I have other coping mechanisms for anxiety, but they are daily activities such as meditation, exercises, outdoor time, journaling… I can talk about them in another post.

Because I have accepted how my brain works and its needs, I can work on actions to help me functioning and do what I want for my life without being held back by my anxiety :)

Have a great Sunday.

entry.title: Dealing with touch
entry.summary: Hello! I can’t believe I am finally back on blogging! I’ve been wanting to come back for some time now, I am happy I found the time to return. For the first post, I will publish a reviewed version of an old post about my “touch sensitivities”. In …
entry.content:
Hello! I can’t believe I am finally back on blogging! I’ve been wanting to come back for some time now, I am happy I found the time to return. For the first post, I will publish a reviewed version of an old post about my “touch sensitivities”. In general, I don’t like when people touch me, but I especially dislike when they do this unexpectedly. For example when someone tries to catch my attention by touching me. I hate it!

To me, these touches are like fire or electricity on my skin and this sensation remains for quite sometime after the touch. It makes me uneasy and for some time, I can only think about this sensation and how it troubles me. When someone touches me, my brain stops, it seems as if I am underwater or I am watching the situation from outside my body. I can’t react properly, I can’t process anymore…I am basically there waiting to return to myself at some point. Usually, I look to the person without reaction. I wish I could react like crazy yelling “don’t touch me!!!” like those funny gifs, but I just can’t. Mostly because my brain stops and when I return to myself the moment already passed.

I can’t handle very well with hugs too. I do like hugs, but I kind of have a limit. If you hug me for too long, it’s awful for me. I feel as I’m suffocating, it seems I just can’t breathe. It happens even with people who I absolutely love and are close to me. Even with my siblings and my parents. My youngest sister and bother love to hug me and I frequently recoil myself under their hugs.

One beloved friend of mine used to like to hug me too long and this would make me feel awkward. After his hug, I would spend a lot of time trying to come back to normal because it really made me feel uneasy.
I couldn’t say it to him because I know he hugged me like that because he was demonstrating that I’m a special friend to him, I didn’t want to hurt his feelings. Same thing with my mom. My mom used to talk with me touching my arm. Oh my goodness, just to remember that make me feel uneasy. When she does that I can’t even understand what she is saying, my brain stops to process any input from my ears. But I can’t ask her to stop to do that. I hate when I hurt someone’s feelings so I prefer to suffer from these things to take a chance of hurting people, especially those who are special to me.

I also have these troubles with my husband. Even though I love his touch and it makes me feel so good, there are sometimes that I can’t handle it. In these times I recoil under his touch. I am a very very lucky girl because he is so understanding, he knows me and sees that when I am like this I need to be left alone for a while and he does that without thinking that he did something wrong. And after some time I go to him and hug him and say: “It’s ok you can touch me now”. One day he even asked me: “I’m going to hug you now, is that ok?”. I can’t express how much it meant to me! I could prepare myself for the touch, it made all the difference. Also, it meant to me that he really understands and respects my needs. Nobody has the right to always touch you, even if this person is really close because your body is yours only.

I hate when people from work touch me. Sometimes someone touches me and I want to lay down in fetal position crying because of the sensation the person leaves on my skin! In these situations, I would go to my husband (I’m lucky he works at the same building as I) and ask him to hold me tight at the same place the touch happened in an attempt to make the bad sensation go away. He helps me to train to say to people don’t touch me, but I haven’t managed yet to express myself in this sense. At least, not at the touching moment…

I used to have a colleague in work that he will forever be my reference on how I wish people treat me. He was absolutely friendly and nice and he never ever touched me, not even once! I love him for that haha. He is the living proof that you can be friendly without physical contact. I love that.

I am training my mind to just say to people to not touch me, but I must confess it is still very hard to me.
